title: Harden desktop native execution and local resource boundaries
forbidden_changes: Weakening URL, path, archive, or component validation
Allowing WebView-rendered content to invoke native commands
Changing public IR or schema contracts
deliverables: Shell-free local path and URL opening
Restricted asset protocol scope and production devtools
Credential-vault-backed Provider secrets with legacy migration
Security regression tests and task report
acceptance_tests: Component removal rejects separators, dot segments, and containment escapes
Malicious Markdown titles are rendered as text during search highlighting
URLs and paths are opened without cmd.exe
Downloaded executable helpers require a pinned SHA-256 digest
Provider API keys are absent from the persisted settings JSON after migration
Focused Rust tests and npm check pass
security_requirements: All recursive deletion targets must remain below their configured root
Untrusted Markdown must not create executable WebView markup
No untrusted value may be interpreted by a command shell
Executable downloads and installed components must be integrity checked before execution
Secrets must use the OS credential vault
